verdicts.reason: TRIGGERS: Criston Cole's appointment as Hand immediately precedes the executions (line 75). Next beat, no decisional mediation.
ENABLES: Cole devises the assassination plan (line 79 quote exact), but chance brings the twins face-to-face; the duel is not forced, only made possible by Cole's strategy.
MOTIVATES: Aegon's Rook's Rest injuries (lines 145-152) motivate Cole's decision to install Aemond as regent (line 153 quote exact). Target is the decision-event.
MOTIVATES: Dragon-loss at Rook's Rest (lines 189-194 quote exact) drives Jace's decision to call dragonseeds. Real causation of the decision, not mechanical force.
SUB_BEAT_OF: Nettles' taming (line 249 quote exact) is one strand within the Sowing event-hub, not an independent causal chain.
ENABLES: The four Sowing-produced dragonriders (line 33 p02 quote exact) make the Gullet victory possible but don't force it; engagement still required.
ENABLES: Queen's dragons (line 131 p02 quote exact) precondition the fall but actual driver is City Watch's defection (line 125). Not mechanical causation.
TRIGGERS: Addam's Seasmoke success (line 235 quote exact) immediately prompts Corlys's legitimization petition. 'Not long after' signals immediate spark.
TRIGGERS: Jace's death (line 41 p02 quote exact) is immediate next beat after Vermax crash, within same continuous action.
VICTIM_IN: Spicetown (line 45 p02 quote exact) is a casualty location of the Gullet battle. Role edge properly wired.
VICTIM_IN: High Tide (line 45-46 p02 quote exact) is a casualty location of the Gullet. Role edge correctly placed.

Type should be ENABLES not MOTIVATES. The Watch's love for Daemon (line 125 p02 quote exact) enables their defection but does not directly motivate the fall-event itself. They are the third party whose free decision produces the fall; their disposition is motivation for their own treason, which ENABLES the city's collapse. The Watch is the actor, not the motivation source per MOTIVATES rubric.
TRIGGERS: Gwayne's death (line 127-129 p02) and gate-opening order in same sentence (line 129 quote exact). Immediate unbroken action.
TRIGGERS: Otto's execution (line 243 p02 quote exact) follows directly from fall as part of immediate account-settling. Same continuous scene.
TRIGGERS: News of fall enrages Aemond (line 173 p02 quote exact); 'First to suffer for it was Ser Simon Strong' (next sentence). Fury immediately produces Strongs' destruction.
TRIGGERS: Willow's seizure of Maelor (line 77 p03 quote exact) is immediate next beat after Thorne's death. Same continuous bridge scene.
KILLS: Disputed per Mushroom (line 81 p03 quote exact). Tier-2 + disputed + in_universe_source correctly flagged. Record confirmed for archive even though edge is dropped as duplicate.

MOTIVATES: Accusation drives Rhaenyra's arrest order (line 107 p04 quote exact: command to Luthor). Addam's escape is cascade from accusation → suspicion → arrest. Accusation is ultimate motivation for the escape decision.
TRIGGERS: Addam's escape (Seasmoke taking flight, line 113 p04) immediately redirects Luthor's anger to Corlys (line 113 quote exact). Same moment, one continuous action.
ENABLES: Kill order (line 109 p04 quote exact) creates threat that enables escape but does not force it; Norren's choice to warn them is what produces departure.
MOTIVATES: Norren's warning (line 147 p04 quote exact) drives Daemon and Nettles' decision to flee at dawn. Free choice against vows, not mechanical trigger.
ENABLES: Aemond's freedom from castle duty (line 109 p03 quote exact) preconditions the eventual confrontation at Harrenhal but does not force it; Aemond's choice to engage remains free.

ENABLES: Nettles' departure (lines 205-207 p04) leaves Daemon solitary at Harrenhal; Aemond himself confirms this as decisive (line 235 p04 quote exact: 'You were a fool to come alone'). Precondition, not forced causation.

MOTIVATES: Daemon's vigil at Harrenhal (lines 215-240 p04) is deliberate bait. Daemon's own words (line 235 p04 quote exact: 'Were I not alone, you would not have come') confirm it motivates Aemond's decision to engage, not mechanical forcing.

new_node_checks.reason: Era/year correct (129 AC per line 251-252 p01). Event documented in lines 205-250 p01. Identity accurate: Jace's call, four dragonriders named correctly, Mushroom/Munkun/Eustace sourced. Genuinely missing from prior graph layers.

Year 130 AC correct. Type event.death appropriate. Identity matches text (lines 60-83 p03): Aegon II's younger son, fleeing with Thorne, seized at Bitterbridge bridge, three conflicting death accounts per chroniclers. Genuinely missing.

Year 130 AC correct. Type event.incident appropriate. Identity matches lines 102-106 p04: Rhaenyra summons Mysaria, accusation against Nettles/Daemon, resulting arrest/kill orders. Genuinely missing node for causal wiring.
